headline: Board action needed to sustain governance gains and unlock ₹500 Cr green bond opportunity
board_paragraph: YES Bank’s recent governance overhaul and RBI-led reconstruction have restored stability, creating a direct ESG-driven upside of ₹4.1 Cr (engine estimate) and opening a potential ₹500 Cr green bond issuance window (engine estimate). The board must act within 12 weeks to maintain reform momentum and capitalize on this opportunity. Failure to do so risks reversal of the ₹4.1 Cr benefit, renewed RBI/SEBI scrutiny, and potential loss of investor confidence, with a canonical exposure of ₹500.0 Cr (REQUIRED).

stakeholder_map.stance: Enforcement escalation if remediation plan absent within 30-60 days. Formal show-cause → interim penalty → appeal window. Settlement via Consent Mechanism in 40-60% of cases.
Direct intervention via moratorium, AQR, or governance restructuring for material asset-quality or fraud events. Climate stress test framework applies to top-16 banks from FY27.

AGAINST vote recommendation on audit committee chair or CEO re-election for material governance failures. Downgrade of governance rating (MSCI/ISS QualityScore) by 1-2 bands. AGM season cadence: April-September.
stakeholder_map.precedent: Infosys 2017 Sikka exit → ISS flagged 2 AGMs

stakeholder_map.stance: Rating action (e.g., BBB → BB) within 90-180 days of material event. Watch-list status in interim. Flow-through to MSCI ESG Leaders / SRI indices triggers 1 (engine estimate)-3% AUM outflow from passive strategies.
stakeholder_map.precedent: Vedanta 2020 B→CCC → MSCI ESG Leaders exclusion

stakeholder_map.stakeholder: BlackRock + Norges Bank IM + CalPERS (Top-10 AUM)
stakeholder_map.stance: Private engagement first (letter/meeting) within 30-60 days. Vote against if remediation absent by next AGM. Exclusion from ESG mandate AUM if severity=high sustained 2+ quarters. Position review on 8-12% AUM flows.
stakeholder_map.precedent: NBIM 2017 excluded 11 coal miners incl. Adani Ports

three_year_trajectory.do_nothing: If no further action is taken, the FY27-29 horizon could see reversal of the ₹4.1 Cr ESG-driven benefit (engine estimate), risk of enforcement escalation by SEBI and RBI, and potential downgrades by proxy advisors and ESG rating agencies. This could trigger exclusion from key indices, 1-3% AUM outflows, and a possible ₹500.0 Cr (REQUIRED) exposure, with credit rating pressure and negative investor sentiment.
three_year_trajectory.act_now: By acting within 12 weeks to reinforce governance reforms and pursue the ₹500 Cr green bond (engine estimate), the bank can secure the ₹4.1 Cr ESG benefit (engine estimate), attract new ESG-focused capital, and maintain or improve credit ratings. The intervention cost is minimal compared to the ₹500.0 Cr (REQUIRED) opportunity, with benefits expected to materialize within the FY27-29 period.

qna_drafts.earnings_call: We have made significant progress in governance reforms, restoring confidence and unlocking a ₹4.1 Cr ESG-driven benefit (engine estimate). Our focus now is to sustain this momentum and pursue a ₹500 Cr green bond issuance (engine estimate), positioning YES Bank as a leader in responsible banking.
qna_drafts.press_statement: YES Bank acknowledges the importance of robust governance and has implemented comprehensive reforms. We are committed to maintaining these standards and leveraging our improved ESG profile to pursue a ₹500 Cr green bond opportunity (engine estimate), ensuring long-term value for all stakeholders.
qna_drafts.board_qa: Q: What is the maximum penalty if we lose momentum on reforms? A: Enforcement escalation by SEBI or RBI could expose us to up to ₹500.0 Cr (REQUIRED) in downside risk, including penalties and lost opportunities. Q: What is the FY26 capex impact of acting now? A: The cost of reinforcing governance is minimal, while the potential upside from a ₹500 Cr green bond (engine estimate) far outweighs any incremental expense. Q: How soon do we need to act? A: Within 12 weeks to avoid regulatory escalation and secure ESG-related benefits.
qna_drafts.regulator_qa: YES Bank is committed to full compliance and ongoing governance improvements. We have implemented board-level reforms and are actively pursuing ESG-linked opportunities. We will provide regular updates and work cooperatively to meet all regulatory expectations within the prescribed timelines.

warnings: verifier: added 3 source tags on ₹ figures
